name: IRC_GV_FIELDS_B
description: This table stores the fields which will be added as part of the grid view  for specific functional area.

columns.Name: FIELD_ID
columns.Comments: Primary key of table. System Generated.

columns.Name: FIELD_CODE
columns.Comments: Stores the code for the field. Field Code can be defined like this ORA_*CONTEXT*_FIELD. For example - Candidate Name Field for Job Application Context , the field code can be ORA_JA_CAND_NAME

columns.Name: CATEGORY_CODE
columns.Comments: Stores the information regarding which category the field points to. CATEGORIES can be ORA_EDUCATION , ORA_EXPERIENCE etc.

columns.Name: GV_CONTEXT_CODE
columns.Comments: Stores the information about the context for which the fields are being stored. For eg- For Job Applications Feature , this column value will be ORA_SUBMISSION and for Candidates  it will be ORA_CANDIDATE_SEARCH

columns.Name: FIELD_TYPE_CODE
columns.Comments: Stores the information about what type of field is being defined. For eg :- ORA_GENERAL , ORA_COMPOUND , ORA_EFF_GENERAL , ORA_EFF_COMPOUND

columns.Name: VO_ATTRIBUTE_PATH
columns.Comments: The full path of the View Object Attribute. Starting from the parent until the attribute. Each level denotes with a dot. For eg , to define degreeName :- SubmissionListVO.SubmissionEducationVO.degreeName

columns.Name: OSCS_ATTRIBUTE_PATH
columns.Comments:
OSCS Index Path for the given field from the Index. Individual fields can be denoted with the index attribute name itself. For eg , if DegreeName needs to be used then this field will have "DegreeName" as a value.
If it is a Compound Field , then the parent node which has these fields would need to be provided. For eg :- if Education Compound Field needs to be denoted then :- JobApplicationEducation

columns.Name: DATA_TYPE
columns.Comments: Stores the data type of the field. The field data type can be - text , number and date

columns.Name: TRANSLATABLE_FLAG
columns.Comments: This column denotes whether the field is translatable in the OSCS Index or DB. If Y , then the field will be used as a translatable field and the corresponding translated values will be fetched from the destined source. If N , then the field will be considered as non-translatable.

columns.Name: MULTI_ROW_FLAG
columns.Comments: This column denotes whether the field is a multi row field or a single row  field. For eg :- Candidate Name is a single row field then the value of this column will be 'N'. But Degree Name is a multi row field , then the value of this column will be 'Y'.

columns.Name: DISPLAY_ROWS
columns.Comments: This column denotes how many rows does user expect to see for this field. For eg :- If 3 is put in , then 3 rows will be shown to the user on the UI related to the given field.

columns.Name: HIDDEN_FLAG
columns.Comments: This column denotes whether the field needs to be hidden on the configuration screen or not.

columns.Name: CUSTOM_PROPERTIES
columns.Comments: This column will be used to store the custom properties of a field. For eg :- Storing Sort field etc.

columns.Name: MODULE_ID
columns.Comments: Seed Data Framework: indicates the module that owns the row. A module is an entry in Application Taxonomy such as a Logical Business Area. When the MODULE_ID column exists and the owner of the row is not specified, then the Seed Data Framework will not extract the row as seed data.

columns.Name: OBJECT_VERSION_NUMBER
columns.Comments: Used to implement optimistic locking. This number is incremented every time that the row is updated. The number is compared at the start and end of a transaction to detect whether another session has updated the row since it was queried.

columns.Name: CREATED_BY
columns.Comments: Who column: indicates the user who created the row.

columns.Name: CREATION_DATE
columns.Comments: Who column: indicates the date and time of the creation of the row.

columns.Name: LAST_UPDATED_BY
columns.Comments: Who column: indicates the user who last updated the row.

columns.Name: LAST_UPDATE_DATE
columns.Comments: Who column: indicates the date and time of the last update of the row.

columns.Name: LAST_UPDATE_LOGIN
columns.Comments: Who column: indicates the session login associated to the user who last updated the row.

columns.Name: SEED_DATA_SOURCE
columns.Comments: Source of seed data record. A value of 'BULK_SEED_DATA_SCRIPT' indicates that record was bulk loaded. Otherwise, specifies the name of the seed data file.

columns.Name: ORA_SEED_SET1
columns.Comments: Oracle internal use only. Indicates the edition-based redefinition (EBR) context of the row for SET1. Context values are Y or N.

columns.Name: ORA_SEED_SET2
columns.Comments: Oracle internal use only. Indicates the edition-based redefinition (EBR) context of the row for SET2. Context values are Y or N.
